runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 3 in a U.S. microsimulation model, measuring how primary earners in decile 3 respond to bounded relative changes in the effective marginal wage.
citations: Manski-style sensitivity/behavioral elasticities in microsimulation (recall anchor)

runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 3 in a U.S. microsimulation model.
citations: Labor supply elasticity estimates often vary by income/earnings groups
Substitution effects in tax–benefit microsimulations are typically smaller than Frisch or uncompensated labor-supply elasticities for many income groups
Bounded responsiveness specifications tend to produce modest elasticities away from top earners
runs.reasoningSummary: I expect the substitution-response elasticity for primary earners in mid-low income decile 3 to be positive but modest, reflecting limited wage responsiveness relative to larger behavioral margins and income variation; hence a median around 0.3 with a right tail capturing less common higher responsiveness.
